title: Engineer whole-grid pit-wave and snapshot-pressure features
summary: F1 strategists explicitly note that race strategy is easy in isolation and difficult because of other cars; box-to-overtake calls and Safety Car windows create clustered decisions.
rationale: F1 strategists explicitly note that race strategy is easy in isolation and difficult because of other cars; box-to-overtake calls and Safety Car windows create clustered decisions. That points to whole-lap field context, not just driver-local state, as a missing signal.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year x LapNumber snapshot, compute only current/past-safe aggregates: share pitting this lap, previous-lap pit count, compound mix, field median and dispersion of LapTime_Delta, field percentile of each driver's TyreLife and degradation within compound, and deviations from snapshot medians. Add lag-1 snapshot deltas to catch pit waves opening or fading.
expected_effect: Captures herd effects, cheap-stop windows and chain-reaction strategy calls that single-car features miss.
risk: Do not leak future laps or post-target information; generate aggregates from the current lap snapshot only and keep CV grouped by race.
